headline: Today's AI engineering radar — 2026-07-14
summary: Today's lead story: "Workload-Driven Optimization for On-Device Real-Time Subtitle Translation" (arXiv cs.CL). Coverage spans robotics_vla, infra_tooling, repos with 419 items scanned and 5 surfaced across sections.

items.title: Workload-Driven Optimization for On-Device Real-Time Subtitle Translation
items.summary: arXiv:2607.09957v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This report studies on-device English-to-Traditional-Chinese subtitle translation for Taiwan under short inputs, short outputs, batch-size-one inference, low latency, and privacy constraints. These conditions limit the value of optimizations designed for long-context or high-throughput language-model serving. Starting from LMT-60-0.6B, preliminary profiling suggests that vocabulary projection becomes a more important decode-time cost after GGUF qu…

items.title: TS-Mask VLA: 2D Temporal-Spatial Masking for Vision-Language-Action Model with Effective Bridging
items.summary: arXiv:2607.09818v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-language-action (VLA) models aim to understand natural-language instructions and visual observations, and to generate and execute corresponding actions as embodied agents. Recently, autoregressive token-based action generation has driven the development of many representative VLA models. However, this paradigm often reduces action generation to next-token prediction, thereby lacking explicit modeling of the spatiotemporal structure of actio…

items.title: More Structure, Not More Capacity: Object-Centric Representations for Visuomotor Imitation Learning
items.summary: arXiv:2607.09825v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Robotic manipulation policies rely on pre-trained vision models that give either a global scene embedding or a dense patch grid. Both mix task-relevant and task-irrelevant features. Object-centric slot representations are a structured alternative: they group features into a few per-object slots. We test what this structure buys on ManiSkill3 PickCube-v1, with a frozen encoder and a held-out-seed evaluation. Holding the policy, goal token, renderin…

items.title: A Numerically-Robust ROS 2 Port of iG-LIO: Diagnosing and Fixing Toolchain-Induced Failures in Incremental GICP LiDAR-Inertial Odometry
items.summary: arXiv:2607.09947v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: iG-LIO is a tightly-coupled LiDAR-inertial odometry system fusing generalized-ICP and point-to-plane constraints in an iterated error-state Kalman filter over an incremental voxel map. We report an open-source ROS 2 Jazzy port of the original ROS 1 implementation and, more importantly, the diagnosis of environment-induced numerical failures that appear only after the port: a mechanically faithful migration -- estimation mathematics left unchanged…

items.title: IKS Health Beefs Up Its Tech Stack with $557M Acquisition of TruBridge
items.summary: IKS Health acquired TruBridge for $557 million, folding the rural-hospital-focused EHR and revenue cycle company into its AI-powered care platform. It’s IKS’ third acquisition this year. The post IKS Health Beefs Up Its Tech Stack with $557M Acquisition of TruBridge appeared first on MedCity News .

items.title: Europe Posted Its Strongest Venture Funding Quarter In 4 Years As UK Gains, M&A Holds Up
items.summary: In Q2, Europe posted its strongest quarter in four years for venture funding, Crunchbase data shows. All told, Europe-based startups raised $24 billion in the just-ended quarter, up around a third quarter over quarter and two-thirds higher than the $14.4 billion raised in Q2 2025.

items.title: Position: Every Ground Truth is a Human Construction, not an Objective Truth
items.summary: arXiv:2607.09668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Ground truth datasets play a fundamental role as reference values in the training and evaluation of machine learning models. This position paper argues that ground truths are not neutral objective measurements that are naturally given, but instead that they are constructed by arrangements of humans and technologies. We argue that the ML community will benefit from articulating and discussing these often invisible or unreported choices and acknowle…
